item.title: NVIDIA NVLink: The Scale-Up Network for AI Factories | NVIDIA Technical Blog
item.description: The demand for AI continues to accelerate. Workloads are getting larger, models are becoming more complex, and there is mounting pressure to deploy AI compute infrastructure faster than ever.

item.title: At SIGGRAPH, NVIDIA Advances Graphics and Simulation With Agentic and Physical AI
item.description: From open models to real-time simulation, AI and graphics breakthroughs are transforming media, content creation and robotics.

item.title: Integrate NVIDIA Omniverse RTX Sensor Simulation Into Existing Apps | NVIDIA Technical Blog
item.description: Developers building 3D, design, simulation, robotics, and industrial digital twin applications need ways to bring physical AI capabilities into the tools and services they already use.

item.title: Bristol Myers Squibb Building Life Science Industry’s Most Advanced AI Factory on NVIDIA Vera Rubin
item.description: The pharmaceutical company has demonstrated how AI can transform how drugs discovery and is now scaling AI across R&D to accelerate the discovery of next-generation medicines.

item.title: NVIDIA Vera Rubin Maximizes Intelligence per Dollar for Post-Training Workloads — a Key Metric for Agentic AI
item.description: Lowest cost per token from extreme codesign maximizes intelligence per dollar for post-training in the agentic era.

item.title: Q&A: How Capcom Brought Path Tracing to RE ENGINE Across PRAGMATA and Resident Evil Requiem | NVIDIA Technical Blog
item.description: Capcom’s RE ENGINE team set out to bring path tracing into two shipping titles at once, Resident Evil Requiem and PRAGMATA, each with a different visual identity. Over two years, the RE ENGINE team…

item.title: Integrating Context-Aware Video AI Agents Into Enterprise Workflows | NVIDIA Technical Blog
item.description: A video analytics AI agent that can perceive, reason, and act based on massive amounts of video footage must be integrated with existing workflows and applications to be useful.

item.title: Scaling Agentic AI Factories Through Extreme Co-Design with NVIDIA BlueField | NVIDIA Technical Blog
item.description: Agentic AI changes the infrastructure pattern for AI factories. One request can trigger many model calls, tool calls, memory lookups, policy checks, storage accesses, and network transfers before a…

item.title: Sharpen the Sword, Skip the Downloads — ‘Onimusha: Way of the Sword’ Is Coming to GeForce NOW
item.description: Plus, GeForce NOW launches in India moving from beta to public availability, and delivers ‘Denshattack!’ alongside five new games this week.

item.title: Google DeepMind and Isomorphic Labs approach to bioresilience
item.description: Google DeepMind and Isomorphic Labs approach to bioresilience, using AI models to support prevention, detection and response.

item.title: Connect more of your apps to Search
item.description: You’ll be able to securely link and interact with your go-to services directly in AI Mode.

item.title: Create, edit and star in videos with two Google Vids updates
item.description: Gemini Omni and personal avatars in Google Vids make video creation easier than ever.

item.title: NVIDIA Introduces New Jetson Thor Computers to Advance Mainstream Robotics and Edge AI
item.description: New NVIDIA Blackwell-powered T3000 and T2000 modules, paired with new NVIDIA Jetson software memory optimization and agent skills, help partners and customers move advanced robotics, visual AI and edge workloads onto compact, power-efficient systems.

item.title: Build a Multi-Camera 3D Tracking Application with NVIDIA DeepStream 9.1 Skills | NVIDIA Technical Blog
item.description: Developers building video analytics applications across large spaces must track the same object as it moves between camera views. Single-camera 2D tracking lacks reliable depth information and…

item.title: Develop Lightweight USD Runtimes Faster with AI Agents | NVIDIA Technical Blog
item.description: OpenUSD is an open, extensible framework that provides a common scene description language for physical AI. It enables teams to bring CAD data, simulation assets, and real-world telemetry into a…

item.title: Building Faster Cryptography with Carryless Multiplication in NVIDIA CUDA 13.3 | NVIDIA Technical Blog
item.description: For over fifteen years, x86 CPUs have shipped with a dedicated hardware instruction for carryless multiplication. It’s a small but stubborn primitive that sits underneath authenticated encryption…

item.title: NVIDIA and Japan Bring Full-Stack AI and Robotics to Every Industry
item.description: NVIDIA and its partners in Japan are this week showcasing the AI ecosystem's latest advancements. Check back here for updates.

item.title: Lessons From the Leaderboard: What 5,000+ Kagglers Taught Us About Improving AI Reasoning | NVIDIA Technical Blog
item.description: The NVIDIA Nemotron Model Reasoning Challenge invited the Kaggle community to explore a focused question: What techniques can improve reasoning accuracy when everyone starts from the same open model…

item.title: Nemotron Labs: How Open Models Give Enterprises and Nations AI They Can Trust, Control and Customize
item.description: Learn how NVIDIA Nemotron open models help enterprises build specialized AI they can trust, control and customize with accuracy, efficiency and flexibility.

item.title: How to Run an Autoresearch Workflow with RL Agent Skills and NVIDIA NeMo | NVIDIA Technical Blog
item.description: Coding AI agents are becoming practical operators for long-running machine learning (ML) workflows. They can inspect repositories, set up runtimes, resolve build issues, launch experiments…

item.title: Why Performance per Watt Is the Ultimate Metric for AI Infrastructure Efficiency
item.description: From benchmark to production, NVIDIA Blackwell NVL72 delivers the highest performance per watt to maximize revenue and the lowest token cost to maximize profit margins.

item.title: AI in Indian Education: Atal Innovation Mission and Google launch ATL Saathi
item.description: Atal Innovation Mission launches ATL Saathi, a Gemini powered AI assistant empowering India's educators to nurture the next generation of innovators.

item.title: Celebrating 25 years of visual search innovation
item.description: Google Images is turning 25. Here’s a look back at some major milestones — and new ways to explore and create visual content.

item.title: Scaling cryptographic verification to boost computer security
item.description: Cryptographic code supports vital protections in modern computing systems. Learn how a new method helps verify code as developers write it while preserving speed and adaptability as it gets implemented and evolves:

item.title: Amazon and University of Michigan give robots a sense of touch
item.description: HydroShear, a new physics-based simulator, teaches robots how to use their sense of touch to perform complex manipulation tasks, in a way that transfers seamlessly to the real world.

item.title: Aurora 1.5: Extending open foundation models for weather and Earth-system applications - Microsoft Research
item.description: Aurora 1.5 adds 22 more variables, hourly temporal resolution, and probabilistic ensemble forecasting to the Aurora foundation model, making it more useful for real-world weather, climate, and energy applications.

item.title: GeForce NOW Turns Up the Heat With New GeForce RTX 5080-Powered Toronto Server
item.description: Plus, tap into ‘NTE: Neverness to Everness’ with a new update, alongside three new games joining the cloud this week.

item.title: Capturing token IDs during agentic interactions for better reinforcement learning
item.description: A new Rust proxy called Turnstile sits between the model backend and the agent harness to capture information lost in mere text transcripts.

item.title: Create a LangChain Deep Agents Harness Profile for NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra to Improve Performance | NVIDIA Technical Blog
item.description: Agentic systems often face a trade-off between accuracy and cost. The highest-performing proprietary frontier models and harnesses provide top accuracy but are expensive. Fine-tuning offers one way to…

item.title: AI can generate Charts. Flint helps generate better ones.
item.description: Short chart specifications are easy to write, but often produce uninspiring results. Flint is an open-source visualization language that offers a middle path, letting AI agents create expressive charts from compact, human-editable specifications.

item.title: NVIDIA Nemotron Achieves Benchmark-Leading Performance With LangChain Deep Agents Harness
item.description: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra is offering leading performance at lower cost than top closed models with the largest and most widely adopted AI agent orchestration platform. LangChain tuned its Deep Agents harness for NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra, achieving the highest accuracy among open models, while completing more tasks at higher throughput and running at 10x […]

item.title: NVIDIA Vera CPU Boosts AI Factory Throughput to Accelerate Agentic Workloads | NVIDIA Technical Blog
item.description: Agentic systems turn model reasoning into action through multi-step workflows that combine inference, tool use, code execution, retrieval, orchestration, and result handling. As these systems scale…

item.title: Expanding Managed Agents in Gemini API: background tasks, remote MCP and more
item.description: We’re announcing new capabilities in Managed Agents in Gemini API so developers can build reliable, production-ready agents.

item.title: The latest AI news we announced in June 2026
item.description: Here are Google’s latest AI updates from June 2026.

item.title: New York City educators and industry leaders gathered at Google’s offices to shape the future of AI in classrooms.
item.description: Google, the New York Jobs CEO Council and Urban Assembly hosted an AI summit for 150 education and industry leaders.

item.title: SkillOpt turns AI agent skills into trainable assets
item.description: AI agents often fail because their instructions, or skills, are manually modified with no guarantee of improvement. Learn how SkillOpt turns skill editing into a training process, making agent behavior more reliable without changing model weights:

item.title: Unlocking Britain’s next era of productivity: Building a nation of AI trailblazers
item.description: Google UK shares its latest Economic Impact Report and how to enable more people to unlock the benefits of AI-powered technologies.

item.title: Memora scales agent memory to boost long-horizon productivity
item.description: AI agents can't remember past conversations. They must constantly reload or retrieve context, which grows less efficient as tasks get longer and more complex. Memora solves this with a scalable memory system separating what’s stored from how it's retrieved.

item.title: Ask an AI expert: What exactly is the full stack?
item.description: A Google expert explains what it means to take a full-stack approach to AI and why it’s been the foundation of our AI work for so long.

item.title: Turning brain prediction models into testable explanations
item.description: Researchers introduce generative causal testing, which translates black box models into clear hypotheses and verifies them in the scanner, revealing what specific brain regions respond to in language.

item.title: Our latest Google Finance upgrades, including a new app
item.description: The new Google Finance is coming out of beta and launching a new Android app.

item.title: The fuel of the future is already here: Why TRISO matters
item.description: Millimeter-scale particles of nuclear-reactor fuel are encased in four layers of different materials that act as a “miniature containment system”.

item.title: Talos brings continuous genomic reanalysis to nearly 5,000 unsolved cases
item.description: Talos was built to help resolve a major bottleneck in genomic medicine: human review time. The open-source system recovered 90% of in-scope diagnoses while surfacing just 1.3 candidate variants per patient for expert review.

item.title: Boost Inference Performance up to 15x on NVIDIA Blackwell Using DFlash Speculative Decoding | NVIDIA Technical Blog
item.description: As AI systems move from single-turn interactions to coordinated multiagent workflows, low-latency inference becomes increasingly important. Autoregressive LLMs generate tokens sequentially…

item.title: Securing internal systems against increasingly capable and imperfectly aligned AI
item.description: Discover our AI Control Roadmap: a defense-in-depth system to securely manage advanced, potentially misaligned AI agents.

item.title: New research shows how AMIE, our medical AI, could help manage health conditions.
item.description: Research in “Nature” shows our conversational AI system matches primary care physicians in complex disease management.

item.title: Unlocking UK house-building with AI-accelerated planning
item.description: Google DeepMind is working alongside the UK government to co-develop an AI-powered prototype to help cut application decision times by 50%.

item.title: NVIDIA Blackwell Tops MLPerf Training 6.0 with Industry-Leading Scale and Performance | NVIDIA Technical Blog
item.description: NVIDIA delivered a clean sweep in MLPerf Training v6.0, the latest edition of industry-standard AI training benchmarks developed by the MLCommons consortium. NVIDIA achieved the fastest time to train…

item.title: NVIDIA Achieves Leading Agentic Coding Performance on First Agentic AI Benchmark | NVIDIA Technical Blog
item.description: AI agents have fundamentally changed the complexity of inference workloads. Until now, the industry has struggled to define a standard for measuring how inference systems perform under these…

item.title: Inside Project Ire’s discovery of an evasive malware sample
item.description: Project Ire examined a timely malware sample and determined its intent through reverse engineering—identifying LOTUSLITE characteristics even as most major EDR tools did not detect it.

item.title: Google DeepMind and partners announce multi-agent safety research funding call.
item.description: Google DeepMind and partners are announcing a new technical research funding call of up to $10M for researchers worldwide to strengthen multi-agent safety.

item.title: Graviton5’s improved design increases speed and energy efficiency — beyond Moore’s law
item.description: A new chiplet architecture, custom die-to-die connectivity, and support for DDR5-8800 memory and the latest PCIe gen6 interconnects improve performance by 25% for general-purpose and agentic AI workloads.

item.title: EC2’s formally verified “isolation engine” provides mathematical assurance of virtual-machine isolation
item.description: Splitting the “separation kernel” off from the rest of the Nitro security system and using only a subset of the Rust programming language to code it enabled its formal verification.

item.title: Gemini’s guided learning: results from a randomized controlled trial in Sierra Leone
item.description: Google DeepMind shares results from a randomized controlled trial in Sierra Leone, measuring the impact of AI in education on student learning and engagement.

item.title: Real-world grounding in agentic AI
item.description: Four approaches can dramatically improve the performance and trustworthiness of AI agents in operational environments.

item.title: Bridging intent and execution in agentic systems
item.description: The harnesses that mediate between models and tools in agentic systems are becoming their own performance bottleneck, but a few simple design principles can fix what ails them.

item.title: Data Formulator 0.7: AI-powered data analytics for enterprise data
item.description: Data Formulator introduces AI-powered analytics for enterprise data workflows. Data teams can easily bring enterprise data into an AI-ready workspace where users can explore, analyze, and visualize data with AI agents to turn raw data into actionable insights:

item.title: Extending human intelligence through AI
item.description: Understanding AI as an extension of human intelligence—not a replacement for it—offers a more grounded path for building trustworthy AI systems.

item.title: AWS and Gray Lab at Johns Hopkins Whiting School of Engineering announce groundbreaking database for AI/ML antibody design
item.description: The Antibody Developability Benchmark is powered by one of the most diverse antibody datasets, enabling transparent performance evaluation for AI-guided antibody design.

item.title: How AI is changing the nature of mathematical research
item.description: What machine learning theorists learned using AI agents to generate proofs — and what comes next.
